headline: Texas muscle, Ohio State precision reshape Week One power landscape
subhead: Dominant performances from the contenders signal early separation, while one blue-blood stumbles badly.
lead: Week One delivered exactly the kind of clarity that rarely comes in season's first breath. Three of the five top-ranked teams answered the bell with overwhelming force, but the most striking result was Texas dismantling Georgia by 23 points—a margin that suggested something deeper than a single Saturday's work. The pecking order is already shifting.

segments.title: Texas announces itself with sledgehammer efficiency
segments.body: Texas didn't just beat Georgia; it controlled the line of scrimmage on both sides and forced the kind of one-dimensional football that ends seasons. The 37-14 margin flatters Georgia's defensive effort in the second half. This was a program statement that Texas means business in Year One of a new era, and Georgia's offense appeared genuinely discombobulated against a physical, assignment-sound defense.

segments.title: Ohio State's defense smothers Michigan into submission
segments.body: Michigan managed just 19 points at home, and that scoreline doesn't fully capture how thoroughly Ohio State's front seven dictated terms. The Wolverines' offense had no rhythm, no momentum, and no answers. This wasn't a high-wire act; it was methodical defensive suffocation that ranked among the most complete efforts of the day.

segments.title: Tennessee shocks Clemson in Tiger down year
segments.body: Tennessee walked into Clemson territory and left with a 35-20 victory, exposing what looked like significant personnel gaps in the Tiger defense. This wasn't a close contest that hinged on one play—Tennessee's offense found consistent answers, and Clemson's adjustments never came. It's early, but this is the kind of road win that can reverberate through a season's narrative.

segments.title: Penn State, Notre Dame establish Big Ten credentials early
segments.body: Penn State's 34-16 dismantling of Oregon and Notre Dame's 45-28 win over UCLA sent a message that the power poll's top tier isn't a four-team club. Both programs displayed the kind of balanced attack and defensive identity you build a season on. Neither needed a close finish to validate Week One; dominance was the statement.

closer: Week Two will test whether Ohio State and Texas can sustain their brutality against stiffer competition, and whether Tennessee's road victory signals real growth or a one-week anomaly.
